Workout Select — R1

Frame 1 re-clothes today's day picker as an always-visible week list. Frame 2 is the decision aid for the charter's open question: training blocks lost their four hues — A text designation, B a luminance step, and C an accent-tinted marker are the same screen, three ways. Judge: does the week read at a glance without block colors, and which of A/B/C should carry forward into tokens.json — or should two blend?

➜Run
C · accent-tinted marker

Frame 1 trims today's hidden "Change Workout" sheet into an always-visible week list — same day/session data as the current picker, rest days excluded exactly like getTrainingDays(). State is told the same way the logging screen tells it: done reuses the moss ✓ tap-glyph, today gets the accent ring plus a Start Session CTA, upcoming reuses the neutral · glyph — no new vocabulary. Frame 2 is the block-identity decision aid: A/B/C are the identical screen, differing only in how "Strength · Week 5" renders. A costs nothing but may under-signal which block you're training. B moves only through the neutral ramp — zero hue, the purest read of "structure carries hierarchy." C is the most legible at a glance, but it spends the accent hue on identity, so its chip is visually indistinguishable from the "Today" pill and the Start CTA one line below on the very same screen — judge whether that overlap reads as confusing, or as an acceptable second job for the one hue that means now. Pick one, or blend two.
